# kubernetes_gateway (Resource)

Gateway represents an instance of a service-traffic handling infrastructure by binding Listeners to a set of IP addresses.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `metadata` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Standard gateway_v1's metadata. More info: https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--metadata))
- `spec` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Spec defines the desired state of Gateway. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec))

### Optional

- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `status` (List of Object) Status defines the current state of Gateway. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--status))

<a id="nestedblock--metadata"></a>
### Nested Schema for `metadata`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) An unstructured key value map stored with the gateway_v1 that may be used to store arbitrary metadata. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `generate_name` (String) Prefix, used by the server, to generate a unique name ONLY IF the `name` field has not been provided. This value will also be combined with a unique suffix. More info: https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#idempotency
- `labels` (Map of String) Map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize (scope and select) the gateway_v1. May match selectors of replication controllers and services. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `name` (String) Name of the gateway_v1, must be unique. Cannot be updated. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names
- `namespace` (String) Namespace defines the space within which name of the gateway_v1 must be unique.

Read-Only:

- `generation` (Number) A sequence number representing a specific generation of the desired state.
- `resource_version` (String) An opaque value that represents the internal version of this gateway_v1 that can be used by clients to determine when gateway_v1 has changed. More info: https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#concurrency-control-and-consistency
- `uid` (String) The unique in time and space value for this gateway_v1. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#uids


<a id="nestedblock--spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec`

Required:

- `gateway_class_name` (String) GatewayClassName used for this Gateway. This is the name of a GatewayClass resource.
- `listeners` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 64) Listeners associated with this Gateway. Listeners define logical endpoints that are bound on this Gateway's addresses. Maximum 64 listeners per Gateway (Gateway API spec limit). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--listeners))

Optional:

- `addresses` (Block List) Addresses requested for this Gateway. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--addresses))
- `allowed_listeners` (Block List, Max: 1) AllowedListeners defines which ListenerSets can be attached to this Gateway. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--allowed_listeners))
- `default_scope` (String) DefaultScope, when set, configures the Gateway as a default Gateway.
- `infrastructure` (Block List, Max: 1) Infrastructure defines infrastructure level attributes about this Gateway instance. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--infrastructure))
- `tls` (Block List, Max: 1) TLS specifies frontend and backend tls configuration for entire gateway. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--tls))

<a id="nestedblock--spec--listeners"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.listeners`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name is the name of the Listener. This name MUST be unique within a Gateway.
- `port` (Number) Port is the network port. Multiple listeners may use the same port, subject to the Listener compatibility rules.
- `protocol` (String) Protocol specifies the network protocol this listener expects to receive.

Optional:

- `allowed_routes` (Block List, Max: 1) AllowedRoutes defines the types of routes that MAY be attached to a Listener and the trusted namespaces where those Route resources MAY be present. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--listeners--allowed_routes))
- `hostname` (String) Hostname specifies the virtual hostname to match for protocol types that define this concept.
- `tls` (Block List, Max: 1) TLS is the TLS configuration for the Listener. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--listeners--tls))

<a id="nestedblock--spec--listeners--allowed_routes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.listeners.allowed_routes`

Optional:

- `kinds` (Block List) Kinds specifies the groups and kinds of Routes that are allowed to bind to this Gateway Listener. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--listeners--allowed_routes--kinds))
- `namespaces` (Block List, Max: 1) Namespaces indicates namespaces from which Routes may be attached to this Listener. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--listeners--allowed_routes--namespaces))

<a id="nestedblock--spec--listeners--allowed_routes--kinds"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.listeners.allowed_routes.kinds`

Required:

- `kind` (String) Kind is the kind of the Route.

Optional:

- `group` (String) Group is the group of the Route.


<a id="nestedblock--spec--listeners--allowed_routes--namespaces"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.listeners.allowed_routes.namespaces`

Optional:

- `from` (String) From indicates where Routes will be selected for this Gateway. Possible values are: All, Selector, Same.
- `selector` (Block List, Max: 1) Selector must be specified when From is set to 'Selector'. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--listeners--allowed_routes--namespaces--selector))

<a id="nestedblock--spec--listeners--allowed_routes--namespaces--selector"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.listeners.allowed_routes.namespaces.selector`

Optional:

- `match_expressions` (Block List) MatchExpressions is a list of label selector requirements. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--listeners--allowed_routes--namespaces--selector--match_expressions))
- `match_labels` (Map of String) MatchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs used to select by label.

<a id="nestedblock--spec--listeners--allowed_routes--namespaces--selector--match_expressions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.listeners.allowed_routes.namespaces.selector.match_expressions`

Optional:

- `key` (String) Key is the label key that the selector applies to.
- `operator` (String) Operator represents a key's relationship to a set of values.
- `values` (List of String) Values is a list of string values.





<a id="nestedblock--spec--listeners--tls"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.listeners.tls`

Optional:

- `certificate_refs` (Block List) CertificateRefs contains a series of references to Kubernetes objects that contains TLS certificates and private keys. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--listeners--tls--certificate_refs))
- `mode` (String) Mode defines the TLS behavior for the TLS session initiated by the client.
- `options` (Map of String) Options are a list of key/value pairs to enable extended TLS configuration.

<a id="nestedblock--spec--listeners--tls--certificate_refs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.listeners.tls.certificate_refs`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name is the name of the referent.

Optional:

- `group` (String) Group is the group of the referent.
- `kind` (String) Kind is kind of the referent.
- `namespace` (String) Namespace is the namespace of the referent.




<a id="nestedblock--spec--addresses"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.addresses`

Optional:

- `type` (String) Type of the address.
- `value` (String) Value of the address.

Optional:

- `from` (String) From indicates where ListenerSets can attach to this Gateway. Possible values are: All, Selector, Same, None.
- `selector` (Block List, Max: 1) Selector must be specified when From is set to 'Selector'. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--allowed_listeners--namespaces--selector))

### Nested Schema for `spec.allowed_listeners.namespaces.selector`

- `values` (List of String) Values is a list of string values.
